name: AppleScript MCP
what_it_does: Lightweight MCP server that enables AI assistants to execute AppleScript commands on macOS, providing control over native Mac applications like Notes, Calendar, Contacts, Messages, and Finder.
use_cases: Automating macOS application interactions via AI assistants
Creating and managing Apple Notes programmatically
Interacting with Calendar, Contacts, and Messages apps
File system operations via Spotlight and Finder
Remote Mac control via SSH for headless automation
not_for: Security-sensitive environments where arbitrary script execution is a concern
Complex multi-step automation workflows (very minimal tooling)
best_when: You need a simple, lightweight bridge for AI assistants to control native Mac applications through AppleScript, and you trust the execution environment.
avoid_when: You need cross-platform automation, fine-grained permission controls, or are concerned about arbitrary code execution on your Mac.

auth.notes: No authentication required for local use. Remote execution via SSH requires host credentials (user/password) passed in MCP config. No sandboxing or permission scoping - arbitrary AppleScript execution.

pricing.notes: MIT licensed. Fully free and open source with no external service dependencies.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: No documented error handling patterns. The server is under 100 lines of code, suggesting minimal error handling. No structured error responses documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: AppleScript execution is inherently side-effectful. No idempotency guarantees.
known_agent_gotchas: Executes arbitrary AppleScript with no sandboxing - significant security risk
SSH remote mode stores credentials in plain text in MCP config
No permission scoping - AI can execute any AppleScript the user account can run
Minimal codebase (<100 lines) means limited error handling and edge case coverage
No input validation or script sanitization documented

security_score_components.security_notes: Community/specialized tool. Apply standard security practices for category. Review documentation for specific security requirements.
